kstreams/poc-ddd-aggregates/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java source and target version property to 11 to align with the target JDK.
kstreams/poc-ddd-aggregates/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile against Java 8 semantics, potentially missing features or failing to enforce Java 11 language rules required by the migration.

kstreams/poc-ddd-aggregates/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace separate source and target compiler configuration with the unified release flag supported in Java 9+.
kstreams/poc-ddd-aggregates/pom.xml.why_exists: While source/target still works, using 'release' is the standard for Java 9+ to ensure compatibility with the target runtime's APIs (module system). Using source/target with Java 11 might lead to runtime errors if APIs from newer JDKs are used but not available in the target, or simply be considered bad practice for the new JDK.
